title: Unit 3
description: What Is Our World Made Of?

states.title: 🌡️ States of Matter
states.html:
Everything around us is made of matter. Matter can be a solid, a liquid, or a gas.

Solids have a shape. A table, a book and ice are solids. You can hold them in your hands.

Liquids can flow. Water, juice and milk are liquids. They take the shape of their container.

Gases are all around us but we cannot see most of them. The air we breathe is a gas. Steam from a kettle is water that has become a gas.

Matter can change! When you heat ice, it melts and becomes water. When you freeze water, it becomes ice again. When you heat water a lot, it becomes steam!

changes.title: 🔬 How Things Change
changes.html:
Things around us change all the time. Heat can make things change.

When you heat popcorn, the small hard seeds pop and become big and fluffy! When you heat a candle, the wax melts and becomes a liquid.

Cold can make things change too. When you put water in the freezer, it becomes ice. An icicle forms when water freezes as it drips down.

A balloon is full of gas. If you heat a balloon, the gas inside gets bigger. If you cool it, the gas gets smaller.

Ice cream was a liquid before it was frozen. Ice pops were juice before they were put in the freezer. Many things around us were different before!
